text.title: Business Storytelling Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Exec summary spends 200 words on context before stating the recommendation.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treated context as politeness rather than barrier.
antipattern.fix: Move recommendation to sentence 1; context goes in support section.

antipattern.symptom: Body opens SCQA, then switches to Pyramid mid-section.
antipattern.root-cause: Author mixed templates copied from different sources.
antipattern.fix: Pick one framework before drafting; refactor or rewrite if mixed.

antipattern.symptom: Slide lists 'revenue grew 20%' — reader asks 'so?' and tunes out.
antipattern.root-cause: So-what test skipped on each fact.
antipattern.fix: Pair every fact with 'which means...' implication line.

antipattern.symptom: Change announcement reads as a directive; teams react defensively.

root-cause.source: Dan & Chip Heath — Made to Stick

antipattern.root-cause: Pyramid used where SCQA / Pixar was needed.
antipattern.fix: For change narratives switch to SCQA or Pixar; let the audience feel the problem first.
